---
title: "XM vs SK Mapping QC"
output:
  github_document:
    html_preview: false
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 2
always_allow_html: true
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
knit: (function(input_file, encoding) {
    rmarkdown::render(input_file,output_file= 'Readme.md')
        })
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(message = FALSE, warning = FALSE, echo=FALSE)
``` 



# Vergleich SK - XM dataset

# Prepare R
```{r library}

library(dplyr)
library(DESeq2)
library(tidyverse)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggrepel)

```

# Load dds
```{r dds}
# XM_dds_ready <- readRDS(
#   file = "/Users/simonkelterborn/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-Charité-UniversitätsmedizinBerlin/! Files 1/Manuscripts/Kelly hypoxia/XM/dds_filtered_ready_for_DESeq2.rds"
# )

XM_dds_meta <- readRDS(
  file = "/Users/simonkelterborn/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-Charité-UniversitätsmedizinBerlin/! Files 1/Manuscripts/Kelly hypoxia/XM/dds_filtered_with_metadata.rds"
)

load(
  file= "/Users/simonkelterborn/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-Charité-UniversitätsmedizinBerlin/! Files 1/Manuscripts/Kelly hypoxia/XM/deseq2_wgcna.dds"
)
SK_dds <- dds

dim(XM_dds_meta)
dim(SK_dds)

```

# Compare Samples
```{r sample_table}

# =========================================================
# 0) Counts extrahieren und Korrelationsmatrix berechnen
# =========================================================

# Counts aus beiden dds
counts_SK <- assay(SK_dds, "counts")
counts_XM <- assay(XM_dds_meta, "counts")

# Gemeinsame Gene
common_genes <- intersect(rownames(counts_SK), rownames(counts_XM))

counts_SK <- counts_SK[common_genes, ]
counts_XM <- counts_XM[common_genes, ]

# log2-Transformation
log_SK <- log2(counts_SK + 1)
log_XM <- log2(counts_XM + 1)

# Korrelationsmatrix: SK-Samples (Zeilen) vs XM-Samples (Spalten)
cor_matrix <- cor(log_SK, log_XM, method = "pearson")

# =========================================================
# 1) best_matches und match_table bauen
# =========================================================

best_matches <- apply(cor_matrix, 1, function(x) {
  list(
    best_XM_sample = names(sort(x, decreasing = TRUE))[1],
    correlation    = sort(x, decreasing = TRUE)[1],
    rank           = order(x, decreasing = TRUE)[1]
  )
})

match_table <- data.frame(
  your_sample    = rownames(cor_matrix),                         # SK-Sample (mit RNA_-Präfix)
  best_XM_sample = sapply(best_matches, function(x) x$best_XM_sample),
  correlation    = sapply(best_matches, function(x) x$correlation),
  rank           = sapply(best_matches, function(x) x$rank),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
) %>%
  arrange(desc(correlation))

# =========================================================
# 2) Master-Tabelle über alle 88 XM-Samples
# =========================================================

# 2.1 Sample-Namen
sk_samples <- colnames(SK_dds)
xm_samples <- colnames(XM_dds_meta)

core_sk <- sub("^RNA_", "", sk_samples)
core_xm <- xm_samples

# 2.2 SK-Basistabelle
sk_tab <- data.frame(
  core    = core_sk,
  sk_name = sk_samples,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# 2.3 XM-Basistabelle
xm_tab <- data.frame(
  core    = core_xm,
  xm_name = xm_samples,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# 2.4 XM-Metadaten + XM_group
xm_cd <- as.data.frame(colData(XM_dds_meta))
xm_cd$core <- rownames(xm_cd)

xm_meta <- xm_cd %>%
  transmute(
    core,
    condition_XM = as.character(condition),
    genotype_XM  = as.character(genotype),
    XM_group     = paste(genotype_XM, condition_XM, sep = "_")
  )

# 2.5 SK-Metadaten
sk_cd <- as.data.frame(colData(SK_dds))
sk_cd$sk_name <- rownames(sk_cd)

# falls rownames != colnames, auf colnames mappen
if (!all(sk_tab$sk_name %in% sk_cd$sk_name)) {
  sk_cd$sk_name <- colnames(SK_dds)
}

sk_meta <- sk_tab %>%
  left_join(
    sk_cd %>%
      transmute(
        sk_name,
        condition_SK = as.character(condition)
      ),
    by = "sk_name"
  )

# 2.6 Korrelationstabelle vorbereiten
cor_tab <- match_table %>%
  mutate(core = sub("^RNA_", "", your_sample)) %>%
  select(core, correlation)

# 2.7 gemeinsame Kern-IDs
common_core <- intersect(core_sk, core_xm)

# 2.8 Vergleichstabelle für gemeinsame Samples (Difference)
cond_compare <- sk_meta %>%
  filter(core %in% common_core) %>%
  inner_join(
    xm_meta %>% filter(core %in% common_core),
    by = "core"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    SK_expected_XM_group = case_when(
      condition_SK == "Kelly_Nx" ~ "WT_Nx",
      condition_SK == "Kelly_Hx" ~ "WT_Hx",
      condition_SK == "HIF1B_Nx" ~ "HIF1b-KO_Nx",
      condition_SK == "HIF1B_Hx" ~ "HIF1b-KO_Hx",
      condition_SK == "HIF1A_Nx" ~ "HIF1a-KO_Nx",
      condition_SK == "HIF1A_Hx" ~ "HIF1a-KO_Hx",
      condition_SK == "HIF2A_Nx" ~ "HIF2a-KO_Nx",
      condition_SK == "HIF2A_Hx" ~ "HIF2a-KO_Hx",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    Difference = ifelse(
      !is.na(SK_expected_XM_group) & SK_expected_XM_group == XM_group,
      ".",
      paste0("SK:", condition_SK, " | XM:", XM_group)
    )
  ) %>%
  select(core, sk_name, condition_SK, Difference)

# 2.9 Master-Tabelle: alle XM-Samples, SK-Infos wo vorhanden
master_tab <- xm_tab %>%
  left_join(xm_meta,      by = "core") %>%                                     # XM_group für alle 88
  left_join(sk_meta,      by = "core") %>%                                     # sk_name, condition_SK (falls vorhanden)
  left_join(cor_tab,      by = "core") %>%                                     # correlation
  left_join(cond_compare %>% select(core, Difference), by = "core") %>%        # Difference für gemeinsame
  mutate(
    sk_name      = ifelse(is.na(sk_name),      "removed", sk_name),
    condition_SK = ifelse(is.na(condition_SK), "removed", condition_SK),
    XM_group     = ifelse(is.na(XM_group),     "removed", XM_group),
    Difference   = ifelse(is.na(Difference),   "removed", Difference)
  ) %>%
  select(core, sk_name, xm_name, condition_SK, XM_group, correlation, Difference)

master_tab %>%
  kable() %>%
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE, position = "left") %>%
  scroll_box(height = "400px")


```





# Plot counts
```{r counts}

# =========================================================
# Funktion: XM-Counts für ein Gen-Symbol holen + Status
# =========================================================

get_xm_counts_by_symbol <- function(gene_symbol, XM_dds_meta, master_tab) {
  rd_xm <- as.data.frame(rowData(XM_dds_meta))
  
  idx_xm <- which(rd_xm$gene_name == gene_symbol)
  if (length(idx_xm) == 0) {
    stop("Gen-Symbol nicht in XM_dds_meta gefunden: ", gene_symbol)
  }
  if (length(idx_xm) > 1) {
    warning("Mehrere Zeilen für Symbol ", gene_symbol, " – nehme erste.")
    idx_xm <- idx_xm[1]
  }
  
  xm_counts <- assay(XM_dds_meta, "counts")[idx_xm, , drop = FALSE]
  
  xm_df <- data.frame(
    core    = colnames(XM_dds_meta),
    xm_name = colnames(XM_dds_meta),
    counts  = as.numeric(xm_counts[1, ]),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  # master_tab dranhängen: sk_name, XM_group, Difference
  xm_df <- xm_df %>%
    left_join(
      master_tab %>%
        select(core, sk_name, XM_group, Difference),
      by = "core"
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      gene       = gene_symbol,
      log_counts = log2(counts + 1),
      # Status: Konflikt / removed / ok
      is_conflict = !is.na(Difference) & Difference != "." & Difference != "removed",
      is_removed  = sk_name == "removed",
      status = case_when(
        is_removed            ~ "removed",
        is_conflict           ~ "conflict",
        TRUE                  ~ "ok"
      ),
      # S-Nummer aus core extrahieren, z.B. P2041_S39 -> 39
      S_num = sub(".*_S(\\d+)$", "\\1", core),
      conflict_label = ifelse(
        is_conflict,
        paste0(XM_group, "_", S_num),
        NA_character_
      )
    )
  
  xm_df
}

# =========================================================
# Beispiel: CA9 in XM plotten
# =========================================================

df_ca9_xm <- get_xm_counts_by_symbol("CA9", XM_dds_meta, master_tab)

# kurz prüfen, wie viele removed es gibt
table(df_ca9_xm$status)
# sollte u.a. "removed" mit 11 Einträgen enthalten

status_colors <- c(
  conflict = "red",
  removed  = "blue",
  ok       = "palegreen3"
)

ggplot(df_ca9_xm, aes(x = XM_group, y = log_counts)) +
  # Boxplots über alle XM-Samples pro Gruppe
  geom_boxplot(
    aes(group = XM_group),
    outlier.shape = NA,
    alpha = 0.2
  ) +
  # Punkte: nur XM, Farbe nach Status
  geom_jitter(
    aes(color = status),
    width = 0.2,
    height = 0,
    size = 2,
    alpha = 0.9
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = status_colors,
    breaks = intersect(names(status_colors), unique(df_ca9_xm$status))
  ) +
  # Konfliktpunkte labeln mit group + S-Nummer
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
    data = df_ca9_xm %>% filter(is_conflict),
    aes(label = conflict_label),
    size = 3,
    max.overlaps = Inf,
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "XM-Counts (CA9) – Konflikte rot, XM-only removed blau",
    x = "XM_group (genotype_condition)",
    y = "XM log2(counts + 1)",
    color = "Status"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))


# =========================================================
# CA9, LDHA, PGK1
# =========================================================


# 1) Daten holen
df_ca9  <- get_xm_counts_by_symbol("CA9",  XM_dds_meta, master_tab)
df_ldha <- get_xm_counts_by_symbol("LDHA", XM_dds_meta, master_tab)
df_pgk1 <- get_xm_counts_by_symbol("PGK1", XM_dds_meta, master_tab)

status_cols <- c(
  conflict = "red",
  removed  = "blue",
  ok       = "palegreen3"
)

plot_counts_gene <- function(df_gene, title_text) {
  ggplot(df_gene, aes(x = XM_group, y = log_counts)) +
    geom_boxplot(
      aes(group = XM_group),
      outlier.shape = NA,
      alpha = 0.2
    ) +
    geom_jitter(
      aes(color = status),
      width = 0.2,
      height = 0,
      size = 2,
      alpha = 0.9
    ) +
    scale_color_manual(values = status_cols) +
    ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
      data = df_gene %>% filter(status == "conflict"),
      aes(label = conflict_label),
      size = 3,
      max.overlaps = Inf,
      show.legend = FALSE
    ) +
    labs(
      title = title_text,
      x = "XM_group (genotype_condition)",
      y = "XM log2(counts + 1)",
      color = "Status"
    ) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
      plot.title  = element_text(face = "bold")
    )
}

p_ca9  <- plot_counts_gene(df_ca9,  "CA9 – XM-Counts")
p_ldha <- plot_counts_gene(df_ldha, "LDHA – XM-Counts (HIF1-Target)")
p_pgk1 <- plot_counts_gene(df_pgk1, "PGK1 – XM-Counts (HIF1-Target)")

# p_ca9
p_ldha
p_pgk1


```



# PCA Plot

```{r pca}

# 1. Hilfsfunktionen und Farben

parse_group_type_time <- function(group_vec) {
  data.frame(group_raw = group_vec, stringsAsFactors = FALSE) %>%
    mutate(
      treatment = case_when(
        grepl("Nx", group_raw, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ "Nx",
        grepl("Hx", group_raw, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ "Hx",
        TRUE ~ NA_character_
      ),
      genotype = case_when(
        grepl("Kelly", group_raw, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ "Kelly",
        grepl("^WT_",  group_raw, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ "Kelly",
        grepl("HIF1B", group_raw, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ "HIF1B",
        grepl("HIF1b", group_raw, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ "HIF1B",
        grepl("HIF1A", group_raw, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ "HIF1A",
        grepl("HIF1a", group_raw, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ "HIF1A",
        grepl("HIF2A", group_raw, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ "HIF2A",
        grepl("HIF2a", group_raw, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ "HIF2A",
        TRUE ~ "other"
      )
    )
}

geno_cols <- c(
  Kelly = "#B57BB5",
  HIF1B = "#C8922E",
  HIF1A = "#4C4FB8",
  HIF2A = "#4DAF4A",
  other = "grey70"
)

##############################################################
# 2. PCA für SK_dds (nur relevante Spalten aus colData)

# --- SK ---
vsd_SK <- vst(SK_dds, blind = TRUE)
pca_SK <- prcomp(t(assay(vsd_SK)))
percentVar_SK <- pca_SK$sdev^2 / sum(pca_SK$sdev^2)

pca_df_SK <- as.data.frame(pca_SK$x[, 1:2])
pca_df_SK$sample <- rownames(pca_df_SK)

sk_cd <- as.data.frame(colData(SK_dds))
sk_cd$sample <- rownames(sk_cd)

# hier NUR condition holen und klar benennen
sk_meta <- sk_cd %>%
  transmute(
    sample,
    condition_SK = as.character(condition)
  )

pca_df_SK <- pca_df_SK %>%
  left_join(sk_meta, by = "sample") %>%
  mutate(
    core = sub("^RNA_", "", sample),
    group_use = condition_SK
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    master_tab %>% select(core, sk_name, XM_group, Difference),
    by = "core"
  )

parsed_SK <- parse_group_type_time(pca_df_SK$group_use)
pca_df_SK <- bind_cols(pca_df_SK, parsed_SK %>% select(genotype, treatment)) %>%
  mutate(
    is_removed = !is.na(sk_name) & sk_name == "removed",
    is_conflict = !is.na(Difference) & Difference != "." & Difference != "removed"
  )



##############################################################
# 3. PCA für XM_dds_meta (XM_group aus master_tab)

# --- XM ---
vsd_XM <- vst(XM_dds_meta, blind = TRUE)
pca_XM <- prcomp(t(assay(vsd_XM)))
percentVar_XM <- pca_XM$sdev^2 / sum(pca_XM$sdev^2)

pca_df_XM <- as.data.frame(pca_XM$x[, 1:2])
pca_df_XM$sample <- rownames(pca_df_XM)

pca_df_XM <- pca_df_XM %>%
  mutate(core = sample) %>%
  left_join(
    master_tab %>% select(core, sk_name, XM_group, Difference),
    by = "core"
  ) %>%
  mutate(group_use = XM_group)

parsed_XM <- parse_group_type_time(pca_df_XM$group_use)
pca_df_XM <- bind_cols(pca_df_XM, parsed_XM %>% select(genotype, treatment)) %>%
  mutate(
    is_removed = !is.na(sk_name) & sk_name == "removed",
    is_conflict = !is.na(Difference) & Difference != "." & Difference != "removed"
  )

pca_df_SK <- pca_df_SK %>%
  mutate(
    is_removed = !is.na(sk_name) & sk_name == "removed",
    is_conflict = !is.na(Difference) & Difference != "." & Difference != "removed",
    marker = case_when(
      is_conflict ~ "conflict",
      is_removed  ~ "removed",
      TRUE        ~ "none"
    )
  )

pca_df_XM <- pca_df_XM %>%
  mutate(
    is_removed = !is.na(sk_name) & sk_name == "removed",
    is_conflict = !is.na(Difference) & Difference != "." & Difference != "removed",
    marker = case_when(
      is_conflict ~ "conflict",
      is_removed  ~ "removed",
      TRUE        ~ "none"
    )
  )

##############################################################
# 4. Plotfunktion im gewünschten Stil

plot_pca_custom <- function(pca_df, percentVar, title_text) {
  
  # Dummy-Daten nur für die Marker-Legende
  marker_legend_df <- data.frame(
    PC2 = c(NA, NA),
    PC1 = c(NA, NA),
    marker = c("conflict", "removed")
  )
  
  ggplot() +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "grey70", linewidth = 0.4) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = "grey70", linewidth = 0.4) +
    
    # 1) Große Punkte zuerst
    # Hx = gefüllt
    geom_point(
      data = pca_df %>% filter(treatment == "Hx"),
      aes(x = PC2, y = PC1, color = genotype, shape = treatment),
      size = 5,
      alpha = 0.6
    ) +
    
    # Nx = offen
    geom_point(
      data = pca_df %>% filter(treatment == "Nx"),
      aes(x = PC2, y = PC1, color = genotype, shape = treatment),
      size = 5,
      alpha = 0.8,
      stroke = 1.4
    ) +
    
    # 2) Dummy-Layer für Marker-Legende
    geom_point(
      data = marker_legend_df,
      aes(x = PC2, y = PC1, fill = marker),
      shape = 21,
      size = 2.5,
      color = "black",
      stroke = 0.3,
      inherit.aes = FALSE
    ) +
    
    # 3) Ganz am Ende: Marker oben drauf
    geom_point(
      data = pca_df %>% filter(marker == "removed"),
      aes(x = PC2, y = PC1),
      shape = 16,
      size = 2.0,
      color = "blue",
      inherit.aes = FALSE,
      show.legend = FALSE
    ) +
    
    geom_point(
      data = pca_df %>% filter(marker == "conflict"),
      aes(x = PC2, y = PC1),
      shape = 16,
      size = 2.0,
      color = "red",
      inherit.aes = FALSE,
      show.legend = FALSE
    ) +
    
    # Genotyp-Farben
    scale_color_manual(
      name = "genotype",
      values = geno_cols,
      breaks = c("Kelly", "HIF1A", "HIF2A", "HIF1B")
    ) +
    
    # Treatment-Formen
    scale_shape_manual(
      name = "treatment",
      values = c(Nx = 1, Hx = 16),
      breaks = c("Nx", "Hx")
    ) +
    
    # Marker-Legende
    scale_fill_manual(
      name = "marker",
      values = c(conflict = "red", removed = "blue"),
      breaks = c("conflict", "removed")
    ) +
    
    labs(
      title = title_text,
      x = paste0("PC2: ", round(percentVar[2] * 100, 1), "% variance"),
      y = paste0("PC1: ", round(percentVar[1] * 100, 1), "% variance")
    ) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 16) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 20),
      panel.grid = element_blank(),
      legend.position = "right"
    ) +
    coord_equal()
}

##############################################################
# 5. Plots erzeugen

p_sk <- plot_pca_custom(pca_df_SK, percentVar_SK, "SK_dds PCA")
p_xm <- plot_pca_custom(pca_df_XM, percentVar_XM, "XM_dds_meta PCA")

p_sk
p_xm



levels(factor(pca_df_XM$marker))

```
